articles.title: Bhandara: Heat stroke room set up at Bhandara District Hospital; 44 rooms and 168 beds equipped - Lokshahi English News
articles.search_term: (heatstroke OR "heat stroke" OR sunstroke OR "heat exhaustion" OR "heat-related death" OR "heat casualty" OR "heat fatality" OR "suspected heat death" OR dehydration OR "heat cramps" OR "heat illness") Maharashtra
articles.full_text:
Heat stroke room set up at Bhandara District Hospital; 44 rooms and 168 beds equipped
Bhandara: The intensity of the sun is being felt more, causing discomfort among citizens. The heat feels more intense during the afternoon. There is a possibility of temperatures rising further in some areas. Due to a significant increase in temperature, the effects of the sun are becoming more pronounced. The impact of the heat has also increased in rural areas, and signs of heat distress are being observed in some places.
With the increasing severity of heat, the administration is becoming alert. The intensity of summer is increasing day by day, and the risk of heat stroke is being felt. Taking note of this, the district administration has prioritized the health safety of citizens by setting up 44 special rooms and providing 168 beds.
In the last five years, many have lost their lives due to heat stroke, and hundreds of patients have received preventive treatment. Reports indicate that cooling treatment rooms, necessary medicines, trained medical personnel, and services are prepared in hospitals in both rural and urban areas.
Short Summary
The administration has become alert due to the increasing heat
The risk of heat stroke is increasing day by day
44 special rooms and 168 beds available
Cooling treatment rooms operational in urban and rural hospitals
Medicines and trained doctors are prepared
Loss of life due to heat stroke in recent years
